subtopic: IUCN World Conservation Congress and Red List
rationale: Covers the 2025 IUCN Congress resolutions and the latest updates to the Red List, including specific Indian bird species and keystone marine species.
Focuses on IUCN technical commissions (WCPA, SSC), the Kenton R. Miller Award, and the Green Status of species like the Lion.
Groups the UNESCO World Network of Biosphere Reserves (WNBR) with the specific case of the Cold Desert BR and recent state-level amendments to the Wildlife Protection Act.

subtopic: Biological Diversity Act and ABS Regulations
rationale: Details the 2025 ABS Regulations, Digital Sequence Information (DSI), and the statutory three-tier implementation system of the BD Act 2002.
Combines the reintroduction of Cheetahs with Supreme Court directions on Tiger Reserve management and Human-Wildlife Conflict mitigation.

subtopic: International Big Cat Alliance and Small Cats
rationale: Covers the treaty-based IBCA and the specific ecological status of Indian small cats like the Caracal and Fishing Cat.
Compares Asian and African elephants and details the conservation status and habitats of India's three crocodile species.
Focuses on the 2024 population estimates for Gangetic and Indus dolphins and the pan-India assessment of critically endangered vultures.

subtopic: Camel Breeds and Endangered Species of Tamil Nadu
rationale: Details specific camel breeds (Kharai, Bactrian) and the conservation of endemic species in Tamil Nadu like the Lion-tailed macaque.

subtopic: Forest Conservation Act and Green India Mission
rationale: Covers the 2023 Forest Amendment Act, clearance procedures, and the strategies of the Green India Mission under NAPCC.

subtopic: ISFR 2023 and UN High Seas Treaty
rationale: Combines forest/mangrove data from ISFR 2023 with the legal framework of UNCLOS and the BBNJ (High Seas) Treaty.

subtopic: Ramsar Convention and New Sites in India
rationale: Explains Ramsar mechanisms (Montreux Record, WCA) and provides details on newly designated sites in Rajasthan and Bihar.

subtopic: Indian Wetlands and Coral Reef Conservation
rationale: Covers major Indian lakes (Vembanad, Dal) and the distribution and conservation technologies for coral reefs.

subtopic: Island Protection Zone and Blue Flag Beaches
rationale: Details the IPZ regulatory framework for islands and the Blue Flag certification for Indian beaches.
Explains the legal status of Conservation Reserves (Hesaraghatta) and emerging concepts like eDNA, Blue Washing, and De-extinction.
Focuses on the legal structure and appendices of CITES and the statutory powers and legislative framework of the NGT.

subtopic: Global Forest Assessment and UNFCCC COP30
rationale: Covers the FAO's GFRA 2025 and the foundational outcomes of the UNFCCC COP30 (Belem Package).
Details specific COP30 finance mechanisms (TFFF, RAIZ) and international declarations like the Belem Declaration for Green Industrialization.

subtopic: COP30 Health, Oceans, and Methane Initiatives
rationale: Focuses on the Climate and Health Funders Coalition, Oceans Breakthroughs, and the No Organic Waste (NOW) initiative.

subtopic: Green Climate Fund and India's NDCs
rationale: Explains the GCF mandate and provides a detailed update on India's quantitative NDC targets for 2030.

subtopic: Carbon Credit Trading and Green Credit Program
rationale: Covers the CCTS under the Energy Conservation Act and the voluntary Green Credit Program linked to Mission LiFE.
Explains the market-based approaches under Article 6 of the Paris Agreement and the Kigali Amendment to the Montreal Protocol.

subtopic: IMO Conventions and Carbon Capture Technologies
rationale: Details maritime pollution conventions (MARPOL) and the R&D roadmap for Carbon Capture, Utilization, and Storage (CCUS).

subtopic: Glacier Preservation and Climate Tipping Points
rationale: Covers international years for glacier preservation, India's high-altitude research, and the concept of climate tipping points.
Details the nine planetary boundaries and the major global multilateral agreements hosted by UNEP.
Focuses on the GEF as a financial mechanism for five conventions and the WMO's reports on GHG levels and climate status.

subtopic: Energy Transition and Climate Risk Indices
rationale: Summarizes key annual indices: ETI (WEF), Climate Risk Index (Germanwatch), and CCPI.
Covers the latest COP outcomes for Basel, Rotterdam, and Stockholm conventions, and the Minamata Convention on Mercury.
Details the types and impacts of microplastics and the Graded Response Action Plan (GRAP) for NCR air pollution.
Explains the mechanism of cloud seeding, the National AQI pollutants, and the Twomey Effect related to aerosols.
Covers the CPCB's assessment of Polluted River Stretches (PRS) and the comparison of thermal vs. membrane desalination.

subtopic: Industry Classification and Hazardous Waste Rules
rationale: Explains the PI-based color coding of industries and the 2025 Hazardous Waste Amendment Rules regarding EPR.

subtopic: River Cities Alliance and Secondary Pollutants
rationale: Details the River Cities Alliance (RCA) and the chemical nature of secondary pollutants and PFAS.

subtopic: Green Firecrackers and Air Quality Reports
rationale: Covers the composition of green firecrackers (SWAS, SAFAL, STAR) and the findings of the SoGA and AQLI reports.

subtopic: SDG Progress and Financing for Development
rationale: Reviews India's 10-year progress on SDGs and the Sevilla Commitment for financing development.

subtopic: India's RE Status and International Solar Alliance
rationale: Covers India's non-fossil fuel capacity milestones and the ISA's 'Towards 1000' strategy.

subtopic: Solar Technologies and Green Hydrogen Mission
rationale: Details Agri-PV, CSP, and the Green Hydrogen Certification Scheme (GHCI) under the National Mission.
Focuses on the EBP Programme targets, biofuel generations, and emerging fuels like E-Methanol and Bioethanol.
Covers the 2025 National Policy on Geothermal Energy and the financial incentives for coal gasification.
Combines industrial energy efficiency (ADEETIE) with the guidelines for Bio-Input Resource Centres and Natural Farming components.
Details the GIAHS sites in India and the 2025 Environment Audit Rules for industrial projects.
Covers the EC categorization (A vs B), the Great Nicobar Project controversy, and the Dam Safety Act 2021.

subtopic: Draft Seeds Bill and Sand Mining
rationale: Compares the 1966 Seeds Act with the 2025 Bill and details the legal prerequisites for sand mining replenishment.
Covers the RECLAIM framework for mine closure and the shift from Renewable Purchase to Consumption Obligations.
Details the nature of gas hydrates and the membership criteria/reports of the International Energy Agency.
Covers the Coalition for Disaster Resilient Infrastructure and the 15th Finance Commission's methodology for DRR funding.
Explains the IMD criteria for cloudbursts and the NDMA guidelines for tsunami mitigation and oil spill hazards.

subtopic: Oil Spill Technologies and Alert Systems
rationale: Details bioremediation for oil spills and the SACHET/EW4All disaster alert systems.
Covers tropical cyclone terminology, swell waves, and the components of the Disaster Risk Index (DRI).
Details the Global Geoparks Network and the International Seabed Authority's (ISA) exploration contracts.
Explains the Long-Period Average (LPA) for monsoon, reverse faulting, and the significance of the AMOC slowdown.
Covers the mesopelagic zone, polar anticyclones, and the role of the National Centre for Polar and Ocean Research.

subtopic: Geographical Features and Projects in India
rationale: A comprehensive packet covering Indian passes, rivers (Beas, Chenab), and major irrigation/bridge projects.
Groups international geographical features (Davis Strait, Aral Sea) and countries/disaster sites in news.
Focuses on the map of protected areas and specific details on Kaziranga, Mhadei, and Tsarap Chu.
Covers tiger reserves and biosphere reserves in Maharashtra, Kerala, UP, and the Sundarbans expansion.

subtopic: Snow Leopard, Rhino, and Nilgiri Tahr
rationale: Details the conservation status and habitats of high-altitude and montane species like the Snow Leopard and Nilgiri Tahr.
Covers endangered terrestrial mammals including the Asiatic Wild Dog and the Bonobo.
Focuses on avian species and the Dugong (Sea Cow) conservation reserve in Palk Bay.

subtopic: Softshell Turtles and New Fish Species
rationale: Details the Asian Giant Softshell turtle and newly discovered fish species in Karnataka and Kerala.
Covers unique biological entities like Clinidium lalitae, lichens as bioindicators, and tardigrades.
Focuses on endangered plant species including Red Sanders and the White Himalayan Lily.
Covers medicinal plants like Arogyapacha and invasive species like Senna Spectabilis and Conocarpus.
